summary_en: A recap of the 2026 I/O Dialogues, where leaders discuss the future of AI, quantum computing, robotics and creativity.
why_it_matters_zh: 此次论坛汇聚了领域顶尖专家，深入探讨前沿技术的未来趋势，助力行业发展。
why_it_matters_en: The event gathered top leaders to deeply discuss future trends of cutting-edge technologies, driving industry advancement.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Catch up on the Dialogues stage at Google I/O 2026. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

why_it_matters_en: These investments will promote economic development and sustainable energy use in Missouri, supporting future workforce development.

summary_zh: 我们在2026年I/O大会上发布了众多重磅消息，包括顶级公告、产品发布和演示。本文总结了其中的100项主要内容。

why_it_matters_en: These announcements showcase Google's latest advances in AI and related technologies, reflecting the direction of future tech developments.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: 100 things we announced at I/O 2026. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 用Codex构建自我改进的税务代理

summary_zh: 了解OpenAI、Thrive和Crete如何利用Codex构建自我改进的税务代理，实现申报自动化、提高准确性并加速工作流程。
summary_en: See how OpenAI, Thrive, and Crete built a self-improving tax agent with Codex, automating filings, improving accuracy, and accelerating workflows.
why_it_matters_zh: 该项目展示了Codex在自动化复杂任务中的强大能力，有助于提升税务申报的效率和精度，具有重要的实用价值。
why_it_matters_en: This project demonstrates Codex’s powerful ability to automate complex tasks, enhancing efficiency and accuracy in tax filings, which is highly practical and impactful.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Building self-improving tax agents with Codex. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: BrickAnything：具备结构感知标记的几何条件可构建积木生成
title_en: BrickAnything: Geometry-Conditioned Buildable Brick Generation with Structure-Aware Tokenization
summary_zh: BrickAnything提出了一种几何条件自回归框架，能够从多样的3D表示中生成物理可构建的积木结构。该方法以点云作为统一几何接口，通过结构感知树形标记表示积木间的局部连接关系，使序列生成更符合物理构建过程并减少无效状态。此外，引入偏好对齐后训练、有效性约束解码及自适应回滚，提升结构稳定性和几何逼真度。大量实验证明，BrickAnything生成的结构既几何忠实又物理可实现，且标记机制有效降低回滚和重生成。
summary_en: BrickAnything presents a geometry-conditioned autoregressive framework for generating physically buildable brick structures from diverse 3D inputs. Using point clouds as a unified geometric interface, it predicts brick sequences under assembly constraints. The method introduces a structure-aware tree tokenization to model local attachment relations among bricks, aligning sequence generation with physical construction and reducing invalid intermediate states. Additionally, preference-based alignment post-training, validity-constrained decoding, and adaptive rollback improve stability and geometric fidelity. Extensive experiments show that BrickAnything produces geometrically faithful and physically realizable structures, with its tokenization significantly reducing rollback and regeneration compared to conventional methods.
why_it_matters_zh: 此工作融合几何信息与结构约束，用创新的树形标记提升积木序列生成的合理性和物理可构建性，推动了从3D形状到可搭建积木结构自动生成的研究，为计算机辅助设计和机器人搭建提供了新方法。
why_it_matters_en: This work integrates geometric information with structural constraints, using innovative tree tokenization to improve consistency and buildability in brick sequence generation. It advances automatic generation of buildable brick structures from 3D shapes, offering new methods for computer-aided design and robotic assembly.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: BrickAnything: Geometry-Conditioned Buildable Brick Generation with Structure-Aware Tokenization. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 大型语言模型能否自我反省？现实检验
title_en: Can LLMs Introspect? A Reality Check
summary_zh: 本文基于人类元认知研究，对大型语言模型是否能检测并报告其内部状态提出质疑。研究指出，仅凭行为证据不足以证明模型具备真正的自我反省能力。通过分析两种评估范式，发现模型无法可靠区分内部状态被篡改与输入的操作，也无法证明模型对内部表征有特权访问。控制实验中模型表现接近随机，表明当前证据不足以确定大型语言模型具备元认知监测能力。
summary_en: This paper questions whether large language models can detect and report their internal states based on insights from human metacognition research. It argues that behavioral evidence alone is insufficient to prove genuine introspection. By re-examining two evaluation paradigms, the study finds models cannot reliably distinguish tampering of internal states from input manipulations nor conclusively demonstrate privileged access to internal representations. In a controlled setting, models perform near chance, indicating current evidence is inadequate to establish that LLMs exhibit metacognitive monitoring.
why_it_matters_zh: 本文基于严谨的实验分析和对现有评估方法的反思，指出过去的结论可能过早。通过设计更严格的控制实验，揭示了语言模型在自我状态检测上的局限性，对理解大型语言模型的能力边界有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: The paper provides rigorous analysis and critical examination of previous evaluation methods, suggesting earlier conclusions about LLM introspection may be premature. By introducing stricter control experiments, it exposes limitations in models’ ability to detect their own states, which is crucial for understanding the capability boundaries of large language models.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Can LLMs Introspect? A Reality Check. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 智能体记忆是数据库吗？重新思考长期AI智能体记忆的数据基础
title_en: Is Agent Memory a Database? Rethinking Data Foundations for Long-Term AI Agent Memory
summary_zh: 长期运行的AI智能体需要持久记忆，以支持跨会话学习、减少上下文重复输入，并实现对过去决策的审计。目前的智能体记忆系统和数据库范式将记忆视为存储，局限于记录、嵌入或边的正确性，未能满足长期记忆的需求，导致增长失控、语义修正缺失、容量驱动遗忘和只读检索等问题。本文提出将长期智能体记忆视为新的数据管理工作负载，定义了具备状态轨迹正确性的“治理演变记忆”（GEM）模型，取代记录级操作为四种状态级操作，并实现了基于属性图的原型系统MemState，验证了可行性并揭示了与原生引擎的差距，指出三条未来研究方向，构建以记忆为中心的数据管理框架。
summary_en: Long-running AI agents require persistent memory to support cross-session learning, reduce repeated context injection, and enable auditing of past decisions. Current agent memory systems and database paradigms treat memory as storage focusing correctness on records, embeddings, or edges, which only partially meet long-term memory needs, leading to failure modes such as unregulated growth, missing semantic revision, capacity-driven forgetting, and read-only retrieval. This work redefines long-term agent memory as a new data management workload with correctness tied to state trajectory, formalized as Governed Evolving Memory (GEM). GEM replaces record-level operations with four state-level operators: ingestion, revision, forgetting, and retrieval. Six correctness conditions govern state evolution. A prototype system, MemState, on a property-graph backend validates feasibility and highlights gaps to native engines. Three future research directions outline a memory-centric data management workload.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究突破传统将智能体记忆仅视为存储的局限，将长期记忆视为动态演变且受治理的数据管理任务，为构建更持久、可审计、可修正的AI记忆体系提供理论基础和原型实现，推动AI系统在实际长期应用中的可靠性和智能水平提升。
why_it_matters_en: This research challenges the traditional view of agent memory as mere storage and reconceptualizes long-term memory as a dynamically evolving, governed data management task. It provides a theoretical foundation and prototype for building more persistent, auditable, and revisable AI memory systems, advancing reliability and intelligence of AI agents in long-term practical applications.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Is Agent Memory a Database? Rethinking Data Foundations for Long-Term AI Agent Memory. Category: research_papers. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 自我验证蒸馏：您的语言模型其实是它自己的合成数据管道
title_en: Self-Verified Distillation: Your Language Model Is Secretly Its Own Synthetic Data Pipeline
summary_zh: 本文研究了大语言模型（LLM）能否仅利用无标签的提示，通过自我生成并过滤解答，进一步提升自身性能，无需外部教师或工具反馈。提出了自我验证蒸馏算法，模型生成多个候选答案，利用基于提示的三阶段自我验证（循环一致性、事实性和正确性）筛选答案，再用筛选后的数据自我训练。该方法在数学、科学和编码三个推理领域均带来显著性能提升，提升幅度在4B参数模型中最高达16.7个百分点。相较于需要推理时多次调用模型的基线方法，自我验证蒸馏只在测试时调用一次，效率更高。
summary_en: This work explores whether large language models (LLMs) can improve themselves post-training using only unlabeled prompts, without external teachers or tools. They propose Self-Verified Distillation, where the model generates candidate solutions, filters them through a three-stage self-verification process (cycle-consistency, factuality, correctness), and retrains on this curated dataset. Applied to math, science, and coding reasoning domains, this method significantly boosts performance—e.g., a 16.7 point pass@1 improvement in math for a 4B parameter model—while requiring only a single inference call during testing, outperforming baselines that rely on multiple inference calls.
why_it_matters_zh: 该方法展示了语言模型在缺乏标注数据情况下的自我进化能力，降低了对标注数据和外部反馈依赖，提升了模型的自主学习能力和推理性能，对推动自动化模型优化和智能自适应有重要意义。
why_it_matters_en: This approach demonstrates that language models can autonomously improve without relying on labeled data or external feedback, enhancing their self-learning and reasoning ability. It has important implications for automated model refinement and intelligent adaptation, reducing dependence on costly annotations and human supervision.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Self-Verified Distillation: Your Language Model Is Secretly Its Own Synthetic Data Pipeline. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: SPEAR：代码增强的智能提示优化代理
summary_zh: SPEAR是一种结合代码执行能力的自动提示工程优化工具，能自主使用评估、Python编程、设置提示和完成四个工具，通过在沙盒中执行Python代码进行结构化错误分析，从而持续提升下游任务性能。实验显示，SPEAR在多个工业和基准挑战任务中显著优于现有方法，尤其是Python工具对复杂评估起关键作用。
summary_en: SPEAR is an automatic prompt engineering optimizer that integrates code execution abilities, autonomously using four tools—evaluate, python, set_prompt, finish—to perform structured error analysis by running Python sandboxed on evaluation data. This enables monotone improvements in downstream task performance. Evaluations on industrial and benchmark tasks demonstrate SPEAR's superior accuracy, with the Python tool being crucial for handling complex judgment tasks.
why_it_matters_zh: 通过将代码作为操作手段引入提示优化，SPEAR使得优化器能自主编写并执行Python分析脚本，极大增强了其理解错误结构和改进提示的能力，实现连续性能提升。尤其在复杂任务中，Python工具提供了长上下文模型难以直接提取的关键信息，促进准确优化。
why_it_matters_en: By integrating code-as-action into prompt optimization, SPEAR enables the optimizer to autonomously write and execute Python scripts for structured error analysis, substantially enhancing its ability to understand error patterns and improve prompts iteratively. This is especially valuable in complex tasks where key confusion data cannot be reliably extracted by long-context LLMs alone, leading to more accurate and robust optimization.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: SPEAR: Code-Augmented Agentic Prompt Optimization. Category: coding_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

title_zh: 向量并非中立：从导出的大型语言模型表示中推断敏感信息的风险
title_en: Vectors Are Not Neutral: Sensitive-Information Inference from Exported LLM Representations in Summarization
summary_zh: 大型语言模型（LLM）摘要系统可能将私密输入的向量表示传递给下游检索、监控或分析流程。即使源文档受限，导出的向量仍可能在不同访问控制下被使用，从而支持对敏感信息的推断，存在信息泄露风险。研究以临床出院摘要生成为案例，审计了两种导出向量：最终提示令牌隐藏状态和均值池化表示。结果表明，减少一种向量中敏感信息的可恢复性，不一定降低另一种向量的可恢复性。提出的SurfaceLoRA方法针对导出向量进行参数高效微调，降低目标向量中电子健康记录记录的种族信息的可恢复性，同时保持摘要效用，但其他向量的敏感信息仍较易恢复，表明隐私审计和缓解应针对具体导出向量进行。
summary_en: Large language model (LLM) summarization systems may export compact vector representations of private inputs to downstream processes. Even if source documents remain restricted, these exported vectors can still allow sensitive information inference, posing disclosure risks. Using clinical discharge summaries and electronic health record (EHR) race data as a sensitive label, the study audits two exported artifacts: the final prompt-token hidden state and the mean-pooled prompt representation. Findings show reducing sensitive information recoverability from one artifact doesn't guarantee reduction from the other. The introduced SurfaceLoRA fine-tuning method targets the exported vector, reducing race information recoverability in the final-token artifact while preserving summarization quality, though recoverability remains high in untargeted pooled artifacts. This highlights that privacy auditing and mitigation must focus on the exact vector artifacts shared downstream.
why_it_matters_zh: 该研究揭示了即使源数据受到保护，导出的向量表示仍可能泄露敏感信息，强调了在大型语言模型应用中向量安全的重要性。SurfaceLoRA方法为降低向量级敏感信息泄露提供了新的思路，但仍提醒我们需针对具体导出向量进行隐私保护措施。
why_it_matters_en: The study reveals that even protected source data can lead to sensitive information leakage through exported vector representations, highlighting the importance of vector security in LLM applications. The SurfaceLoRA method offers a novel approach to reduce sensitive information leakage at the vector level, yet underscores the necessity for privacy protections tailored to specific exported vectors.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Vectors Are Not Neutral: Sensitive-Information Inference from Exported LLM Representations in Summarization. Category: image_ai. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

summary_zh: Reachy Mini现已支持完全本地运行，无需依赖云端处理。
summary_en: Reachy Mini now supports fully local operation without relying on cloud processing.
why_it_matters_zh: 完全本地化提升了用户的隐私保护和响应速度，有助于更广泛应用于低带宽或无网络环境。
why_it_matters_en: Full local operation enhances user privacy and response speed, facilitating broader use in low bandwidth or offline scenarios.

title_zh: 使用Hub Bucket传输万亿参数：TRL中的Delta权重同步
title_en: Shipping a Trillion Parameters With a Hub Bucket: Delta Weight Sync in TRL
summary_zh: Hugging Face介绍了TRL中的Delta权重同步技术，通过Hub Bucket机制高效传输万亿级模型参数，提升模型更新和同步效率。
summary_en: Hugging Face presents Delta Weight Sync in TRL, a technique leveraging Hub Bucket to efficiently transfer trillion-parameter scale model weights, enhancing update and synchronization performance.
why_it_matters_zh: 随着模型规模达到万亿参数级别，传统的权重传输方式效率低下。通过Delta权重同步和Hub Bucket机制，可以显著减少传输量，提升协同训练和部署的便捷性。
why_it_matters_en: As models scale to trillion parameters, traditional weight transfer methods become inefficient. Delta Weight Sync combined with Hub Bucket significantly reduces transfer overhead, facilitating smoother collaborative training and deployment.
image_prompt: Modern editorial square illustration for an AI news app. Topic: Shipping a Trillion Parameters With a Hub Bucket: Delta Weight Sync in TRL. Category: tools_apps. Clean, premium, technology-focused, no text, no logos.

why_it_matters_en: This partnership enables ChatGPT users to access authoritative Brazilian journalism, enhancing the diversity and reliability of information sources.

summary_en: AdventHealth is using ChatGPT for Healthcare to streamline workflows, reduce administrative burden, and return more time to patient care.
